channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bad algae forming on pond, 11th April
item.description:
Bad green algae developing on pond on Ravensbourne side of the park. Just worried about the baby ducks if it is harmful to them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Greenway, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bright light shining into number 17, 11th April
item.description:
The flood light looks it has been moved again and is shingling directly into our flat.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Braydon Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sweepers’ sacks dumped by tree., 11th April
item.description:
Young Tree will be damaged by sacks left against its trunk. There is no point in sweeping if you are going to leave sacks. It just creates more littering

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 Sacks (originally 4) left by street sweepers for over 3 weeks. Now with faeces adjacent., 11th April
item.description:
The tree outside 29 & 31 Benthal Rd is used by street sweepers as a depot! This is not acceptable. It ENCOURAGES further littering. Pavement at this site is disrupted by tree roots & impossible to clean. Put your sacks on East side of street - more space.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags dumped again, 11th April
item.description:
Bags of rubbish dumped again. Why aren't the council doing anything to catch the perpetrator? It's happening every other day, the neighbours have to live in filth, its unsightly, causes a hazard as its on the path, gets ravaged by foxes and then blows into the road and up the street. Also the food brings vermin.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marmadon Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole opposite 245 Kent House Road, 11th April
item.description:
There’s long been a crack in the centre of Kent House Road in the braking zone as vehicle turn right into Thesiger Road. That crack is now opening up to a pothole and is causing large vibrations to surrounding houses as heavy vehicles pass over the pothole and worse still when they brake. This has multiple issues including damaging to houses and risk of tyre failure and loss of grip in a braking zone near a two pedestrian island crossings. The pothole was marked in white chalk some time ago but nothing has been done and the vibrations are getting worse. The road surface is dangerous and not fit for purpose
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale-Overflowing Litterbin in Front of Maxim Chicken Shop, 11th April
item.description:
Litterbin is overflowing with waste and it needs to be emptied/cleared up.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Jarvis Road-Flytipping, 11th April
item.description:
A trolley has been flytipped and needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Jarvis Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of rubbish, 11th April
item.description:
There is a deposit of a lot of rubbish thrown into the River Quaggy bottom of Mottingham Lane near the collage and the round about also, a bike also a brick of the fence (top of bridge) has been broken and thrown into the river. Also a lot of rubbish on the green area near the river.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mottingham Lane, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This bike has been abandoned for at least 5 years., 11th April
item.description:
Abandoned motor bike has been unmoved for years.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pytchley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Protruding tactile paving slab and broken tarmac chip in Lennard Rd Penge at the corner of Alexandra Rd., 10th April
item.description:
Protruding tactile paving slab and broken tarmac chip in Lennard Rd Penge at the corner of Alexandra Rd. The tactile slab is tilted up by an inch, 2.5cm creating a trip hazard. The knocked-over bollard was repaired only about 3 weeks ago.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lennard Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 10th April
item.description:
More flytipping in same spot council not addressing issues at all. Issue has been reported to local police team and council mutiple times and is still happening

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Rise, Perry Vale, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous potholes in Rectory lane, 10th April
item.description:
These were repaired but unfortunately have re-appeared and case driving around to avoid . There ias a lot in one area
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rectory Lane, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bradenham close SE17 2LU, 10th April
item.description:
This builders rubbish has been left here in a Southwark street outside a Southwark resource centre for more than two years. It looks scruffy and I think it is a hazzard. Also next to this there is a badly broken paving slab by the tree. This is also dangerous. I have reported this three times before.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bradenham Close, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neighbours here treat this area like a bin, 10th April
item.description:
This area near the garages numbers 1-7 phelp st SE17 has just been cleared and already neighbours here have started dumping rubbish already just behind the wall.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 7 Chetwynd Road NW5 1BX, 10th April
item.description:
Hi Camden tree department I live at 7 Chetwynd Road and I’d like to report a dead tree outside our house. One of the branches of the tree fell off last week narrowly missing our car, and I’m worried that with the next strong wind the whole tree could fall. Would you be able to remove the tree and plant another in its place?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chetwynd Road, Dartmouth Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light has exposed live wiring, 10th April
item.description:
The street light at the rear of 103 Kale road has its lower cover missing and there are live wires and connections hanging out
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redpoll Way, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working, 10th April
item.description:
Street light (one on from the bus stop) not working at 21:45 on 10/04/26
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Creswell Drive, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti at 261 Brixton road london SW9 6LH sprayed on a black wooden fence., 10th April
item.description:
I like to report graffiti sprayed on a black wooden fence at 261 Brixton road SW9 6LH. The location is to the left hand side of the Jamm bar. Please can the graffiti be removed. The graffiti has been up for a little over two weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 262, Brixton Road, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped waste by bins, 10th April
item.description:
Please can this dumped large waste be removed swiftly?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-44, Claude Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
